info.name: Microsoft Azure Computer Vision Client
info.description: The Computer Vision API provides state-of-the-art algorithms to process images and return information. For example, it can be used to extract text using Read OCR, caption an image using descriptive natural language, detect objects, people, and more.

item.name: imageanalysis:analyze

Microsoft Azure Analyze The Input Image The Request Either Contains Image Stream With Any Content Type [ Image * , Application Octet Stream ], Or A Json Payload Which Includes An Url Property To Be Used To Retrieve The Image Stream

query.description: The visual features requested: tags, objects, caption, denseCaptions, read, smartCrops, people. This parameter needs to be specified if the parameter "model-name" is not specified.
The name of the custom trained model. This parameter needs to be specified if the parameter "features" is not specified.
The desired language for output generation. If this parameter is not specified, the default value is "en". See https://aka.ms/cv-languages for a list of supported languages.

A list of aspect ratios to use for smartCrops feature. Aspect ratios are calculated by dividing the target crop width by the height. Supported values are between 0.75 and 1.8 (inclusive). Multiple values should be comma-separated. If this parameter is not specified, the service will return one crop suggestion with an aspect ratio it sees fit between 0.5 and 2.0 (inclusive).
Boolean flag for enabling gender-neutral captioning for caption and denseCaptions features. If this parameter is not specified, the default value is "false".

request.description: Needs a more full description created.

response.name: Success

query.description: The visual features requested: tags, objects, caption, denseCaptions, read, smartCrops, people. This parameter needs to be specified if the parameter "model-name" is not specified.

The desired language for output generation. If this parameter is not specified, the default value is "en". See https://aka.ms/cv-languages for a list of supported languages.

A list of aspect ratios to use for smartCrops feature. Aspect ratios are calculated by dividing the target crop width by the height. Supported values are between 0.75 and 1.8 (inclusive). Multiple values should be comma-separated. If this parameter is not specified, the service will return one crop suggestion with an aspect ratio it sees fit between 0.5 and 2.0 (inclusive).
Boolean flag for enabling gender-neutral captioning for caption and denseCaptions features. If this parameter is not specified, the default value is "false".

response.name: Error

item.name:
Microsoft Azure Update The Properties Of An Existing Dataset 
      
status Codes Returned:
 200: Operation Completed Successfully 
 400: The Request Was Malformed 
 404: A Dataset With The Specified Name Was Not Found 
 412: An If Match Header Was

header.description:
Optional ETag for the dataset to update.
If an ETag is provided, then the dataset will be updated only if its current ETag value matches the given ETag. If the ETag values don't match, then the update operation will fail with status code 412 (Precondition Failed). This indicates that the dataset has been updated since the last time information for the dataset was obtained.
If an ETag is not provided or its value is '*', then the dataset will always be updated regardless of the current ETag value.

variable.description: (Required) The name of the dataset to update.

request.description: Needs a more full description created.

response.name: Success

item.name:
Microsoft Azure Unregister A Dataset 
      
status Codes Returned:

 400: The Request Was Malformed

 412: An If Match Header Was Provided, But The Given

header.description:
Optional ETag for the dataset to unregister.
If an ETag is provided, then the dataset will be unregistered only if its current ETag value matches the given ETag. If the ETag values don't match, then the unregister operation will fail with status code 412 (Precondition Failed). This indicates that the dataset has been updated since the last time information for the dataset was obtained.
If an ETag is not provided or its value is '*', then the dataset will always be unregistered regardless of the current ETag value.

variable.description: (Required) The name of the dataset to unregister.

request.description: Needs a more full description created.

This indicates that the dataset has been updated since the last time information for the dataset was obtained.
If an ETag is not provided or its value is '*', then the dataset will always be unregistered regardless of the current ETag value.

response.name: Error
